The task is defective. The verifier.py script is entirely missing from the task environment, meaning no programmatic evaluation or tolerance can be applied.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real dive log data sourced from the official Subsurface GitHub repository (SampleDivesV2.ssrf). However, the task contains several critical defects. First, the setup script (setup_task.sh) fails to strip any GPS coordinates because it mistakenly assumes the source file uses Subsurface v3 XML tags (<site>), when it actually uses v2 (<location>). Consequently, three distinct dive sites natively lack GPS coordinates in the dataset (Sund Rock, Zenobia, and Green Bay), contradicting the prompt's assumption of a single missing site. Second, while the target GPS coordinates (47.3917, -122.5769) are explicitly verifiable from the task instructions, they geographically point to a residential road in Pierce County, WA, rather than the actual Sund Rock marine preserve. Finally, the task is structurally unscorable because verifier.py is entirely missing from the environment directory.
